one_line_description: CPaaS (formerly Nexmo) offering SMS, voice, video, verify, and network APIs.
auth_summary: APIs authenticate either with the account API key + secret (Base64-encoded in a Basic Authorization header, or as query/body params on older APIs) or with JWTs signed by a Vonage Application's private key; which method applies varies per API (e.g. Verify v2 and Voice favor JWT).
access_notes: Self-serve signup on the Vonage dashboard issues an API key and secret immediately, with free trial credit; applications (for JWT auth) are also created self-serve in the dashboard. Some network APIs require additional enablement.
api_surface_summary: REST APIs for SMS, Messages (WhatsApp/Viber/MMS), Voice, Verify, Number Insight, Numbers provisioning, Video, and newer network APIs, with SDKs across major languages.
agent_notes: The specific auth doc URL I tried 404'd, but multiple official developer.vonage.com pages surfaced in search confirm API key/secret Basic auth plus application JWT auth. Vonage publishes official MCP servers (documentation MCP under the Vonage org; server/tooling MCPs announced on Postman) and community telephony MCP under Vonage-Community. Per-API auth-method variation is the main integration nuance.
